name: Epinephrine
executiveSummary: Sympathomimetic agent. Cardiogenic or septic shock with refractory hypotension and low peripheral resistance.
indications:
## Main indications

Sympathomimetic agent. Cardiogenic or septic shock with refractory hypotension and low peripheral resistance.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: 1 mg/mL ampoule (1:1000) or 0.1 mg/mL (1:10,000) per institutional formulation.
adulto.dose: Cardiac arrest IV/IO: 1 mg every 3–5 min. Anaphylaxis IM: 0.3–0.5 mg; repeat every 5–15 min if persistent.
adulto.administration: IV/IO in cardiac arrest; IM in anaphylaxis.
adulto.notes: Record time, dose, route, and hemodynamic response.

pediatrico.diluent: Cardiac arrest: 0.9% NaCl, 5% dextrose. Anaphylaxis: undiluted.
pediatrico.finalConcentration: Cardiac arrest: 0.1 mg/mL (dilute one ampoule to 10 mL). Anaphylaxis: 0.01 mg/kg.
pediatrico.dose: 0.05–1 µg/kg/min, maximum 2 µg/kg/min. Adults: start 4 µg/min; infusion 8–12 µg/min
pediatrico.notes: Oxidizes rapidly; do not use if brown discoloration is present. Saline dilution is not recommended. Norepinephrine effects may be increased with tricyclic antidepressants, antihistamines, and beta-blockers.

neonatal.presentation: 0.1 mg/mL ampoule (1:10,000) preferred for neonates when available.
neonatal.dose: Neonatal resuscitation: 0.01–0.03 mg/kg IV/IO/endotracheal per current NRP algorithm.
neonatal.administration: Umbilical, peripheral, or other access as appropriate.

stability:
## Pediatric guide

- Degrades quickly. Do not use if solution is discolored or precipitated. Discard remainder.

## General

- Protect from light. Store at 15–30 °C.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Hypertension, necrosis, bradycardia. Prolonged use: decreased cardiac output, plasma volume depletion, severe peripheral and visceral vasoconstriction. Stress cardiomyopathy may also occur.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — NORepinephrine (code 1401, ATC C01CA).
